text:
$GOOGL Waymo just unveiled its first-ever national TV commercial. 

Waymo says its autonomous driver is statistically 10x safer than a human driver in the cities it serves, built to never blink, tire or get distracted

$ORCL says its cloud team will be among the first to bring up $NVDA Vera Rubin NVL72.

Oracle says the goal is to deliver the infrastructure layer for the agentic AI era

Anthropic is reportedly moving toward its first direct data center leases with more than a dozen LOIs signed with U.S. developers.

$GOOGL may financially guarantee part of those lease obligations helping Anthropic move closer to the $META and OpenAI infrastructure playbook.
BlackRock reportedly placed an order to buy at least $5B worth of $SPCX shares.

$TE is trying to become the face of American solar manufacturing.

Its Stage 5 process adds American-made steel frame, sealant and junction box that help each solar module survive decades of weather and keep producing reliable power.

OpenAI agreed to acquire Ona which is a cloud startup that builds secure persistent environments for AI agents.

The team will fold into Codex which now has more than 5M weekly users as OpenAI moves faster to turn coding agents into long-running workflows.

Jeff Bezos AI startup Prometheus is reportedly worth $41B and aims to bring AI to engineering and manufacturing.

Bezos says the company has raised significant funding because the work is “very compute-intensive” and said Prometheus could eventually become an $AMZN AWS customer.

Oppenheimer initiated $SPCX with an Outperform rating and a $190 price target

The firm sees SpaceX using its reusable rocket monopoly, Starlink network, satellites, solar panels, chips and manufacturing scale to become the world’s largest communications and AI company.

I think the $ORCL earnings has two truths at the same time.

The first truth is that Oracle’s AI demand is real, massive and still accelerating but the second truth is that Oracle’s AI demand is expensive to deliver which meansthe stock now comes down to capex, financing, margins and dilution.

The opportunity is massive because with ~$638B of backlog, Oracle now has the largest contracted backlog in tech and Larry Ellison is clearly betting the company’s future on OCI.

You can watch my full $ORCL earnings breakdown here:

piped.video/Ur1wVAnc-Jk?si=YDkW…
$ORCL is down nearly 13% as the company plans to raise $40B to fund its AI buildout.

$NOW and $IBM are expanding their partnership to help enterprises unlock AI across legacy apps, messy data and IT operations.

The collaboration combines ServiceNow AI Platform with IBM watsonx and automation tools with new solutions expected in 2H26.

$GOOGL is reportedly in talks with Samsung to make the memory I/O die for Google’s 10th-generation TPU planned for 2028.

$TSM would still build the main compute engine but Samsung could win a key 2nm role as AI chip demand pushes Google to diversify beyond TSMC.

AI data centers are getting bigger, and so are their power needs...

xAI is building some of the world’s largest AI supercomputers, and the energy needed to run them is growing just as fast.

The chart shows how power deman increases as AI clusters get bigger. Meta’s 129,000GPU cluster uses about 165 MW of power, while xAI’s planned Colossus 2 system is expected to reach 550,000 GPUs and 1,000 MW.

Bigger data centers mean higher chip demand and more chips consume more power.

Does the market fully appreciate what that could mean for SpaceX's future growth?
The idea that the market is selling off to make room for SpaceX is fucking stupid.

Be not afraid.

I soon will become annoying as fuck.
